title: Agentic Features — Interview Prep Auto-Trigger UI & Agent Transparency
description: Surface AI agent activity to users: show when interview prep was auto-generated, indicate which apps were auto-tracked from email, and add visual indicators that make the autonomous agents visible and trustworthy.

userStories.title: Interview prep auto-generated badge on ApplicationRow
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/components/ApplicationRow.jsx`, when `application.interview_prep_briefing` is truthy (auto-generated prep briefing exists), show a small badge or indicator. Add a Sparkles icon (import from 'lucide-react/dist/esm/icons/sparkles') next to the company name or in the actions area. Wrap it in a Tooltip with text 'Interview prep ready'. The icon should use `text-primary` color and be size h-3.5 w-3.5. Check the row layout to find the best location (near the stage pill or in the info row).
acceptanceCriteria: Sparkles icon visible on rows where interview_prep_briefing is truthy
Icon wrapped in Tooltip with text 'Interview prep ready'
Icon is aria-hidden='true', Tooltip provides accessible label
No layout shift for rows without briefing
Direct import: lucide-react/dist/esm/icons/sparkles

userStories.title: Interview prep auto-generated notice in DetailPanel
userStories.description: Find where interview prep is displayed in the detail panel (search for 'interview_prep' or 'PrepSection' in frontend/src/components/). When `application.interview_prep_briefing` exists AND `application.interview_prep_generated_at` exists, show a small notice above the prep section saying 'Auto-generated when interview invite detected'. Style it with a Sparkles icon (text-primary, h-3.5 w-3.5), inline text-xs text-muted-foreground. This communicates the agent acted autonomously.
acceptanceCriteria: Notice shown only when both interview_prep_briefing and interview_prep_generated_at exist
Text: 'Auto-generated when interview invite detected' with Sparkles icon
Notice styled as text-xs text-muted-foreground with flex items-center gap-1.5
Sparkles icon aria-hidden='true'
No changes to existing prep content

userStories.title: ConnectedState — add agent status summary line
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/components/SettingsIntegrationsSection.jsx` `ConnectedState` (line 53), the connected card shows email and timestamps but no agent activity summary. Add a text line below the email/connected_at info block (inside the first card, after line 89): if `status.apps_tracked > 0`, show 'Your agent has tracked {apps_tracked} application{s} automatically.' with a Sparkles icon. Import Sparkles from lucide-react directly. This tells users the agent is actively working.
acceptanceCriteria: When apps_tracked > 0, summary line shown with Sparkles icon
Text pluralized correctly (application vs applications)
Icon is text-primary h-3.5 w-3.5 aria-hidden='true'
When apps_tracked === 0, no extra line shown
Renders inside the Connected card below the email info block

userStories.title: Dashboard — Gmail connected banner shows agent activity
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/components/InboxSetupBanner.jsx`, when `status?.connected` is true (and banner would normally hide), instead of returning null, show a minimal 'agent active' indicator — a slim banner or a small chip: a Sparkles icon + 'Email agent active' text + '({apps_tracked} apps tracked)' if apps_tracked > 0. This should be subtle: use `text-xs text-muted-foreground` with no border, just a flex row in the same mb-4 space. If the user has dismissed the banner, still show the agent-active chip (use a separate dismissed state key or override). Actually: only show the agent-active line if status.apps_tracked > 0 AND status.connected. Keep it minimal.
acceptanceCriteria: When status.connected and status.apps_tracked > 0, show agent-active chip
Chip shows: Sparkles icon + 'Email agent active · {N} apps tracked'
Chip styled as text-xs text-muted-foreground flex items-center gap-1.5 mb-2
Sparkles icon is text-primary h-3 w-3 aria-hidden='true'
Original banner (for disconnected state) behavior unchanged
No banner shown when apps_tracked === 0 to avoid noise

userStories.title: Interview prep auto-trigger setting label improvement
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/components/SettingsIntegrationsSection.jsx` ToggleRow for interview_prep (line 114-121), the label says 'Auto-generate interview prep' and description 'Start a briefing the moment an interview invite arrives'. Add context about what the agent does: update description to 'The prep agent researches the company and role automatically — ready before your first interview.' This better communicates the agentic nature of the feature.
acceptanceCriteria: interview_prep ToggleRow description updated to the new copy
Label unchanged: 'Auto-generate interview prep'
No other changes

userStories.title: Settings Integrations page — add 'How it works' section
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/components/SettingsIntegrationsSection.jsx` `DisconnectedState` (line 149), add a 'How it works' steps section before the Connect button. Show 3 steps: (1) 'Connect your job-search Gmail' with Mail icon; (2) 'Agent reads job emails automatically' with Sparkles icon; (3) 'Dashboard updates without lifting a finger' with Zap icon. Use a simple numbered step list with icons. Import Sparkles and Zap from lucide-react directly. Steps should be text-sm text-muted-foreground.
acceptanceCriteria: 3-step 'How it works' section added to DisconnectedState before the Connect button
Each step has an icon (Mail, Sparkles, Zap) aria-hidden='true' and step text
Direct lucide-react imports: .../icons/sparkles, .../icons/zap
Steps are visually distinct (numbered or icon-labeled)
DisconnectedState layout still centers the Connect button

userStories.title: ApplicationRow — email source icon tooltip
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/components/ApplicationRow.jsx`, check how the SOURCE_ICONS are rendered in the row. If the source icon renders without a tooltip, find where it's used in the JSX and wrap the Mail icon (email source) in a Tooltip with content 'Auto-tracked from Gmail'. For other sources keep existing behavior. Search for SOURCE_ICONS usage in the file to find the render location.
acceptanceCriteria: Mail icon for email-source apps has Tooltip with text 'Auto-tracked from Gmail'
Other source icons unchanged
Tooltip uses existing Tooltip/TooltipTrigger/TooltipContent pattern
Source icon remains aria-hidden='true'

userStories.title: GmailCallback page — replace no-op with loading indicator
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/pages/GmailCallback.jsx` (line 6-14), the component returns null while `useEffect` fires the navigate. This means the page is blank for a brief moment. Replace `return null` with a centered loading spinner: a div with `flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen`, containing a Loader2 icon (import from 'lucide-react/dist/esm/icons/loader-2') with `className='h-8 w-8 animate-spin text-primary'` and a `Connecting Gmail…` for screen readers.
acceptanceCriteria: GmailCallback renders a centered Loader2 spinner instead of null
Loader2 has animate-spin text-primary h-8 w-8
sr-only span reads 'Connecting Gmail…'
useEffect navigate behavior unchanged
Direct import: lucide-react/dist/esm/icons/loader-2
